skill.name: raffle-winner-picker
skill.description: Picks random winners from lists, spreadsheets, or Google Sheets for giveaways, raffles, and contests. Ensures fair, unbiased selection with transparency.
skill.summary: Picks random winners from lists, spreadsheets, or Google Sheets for giveaways, raffles, and contests...
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives caused by markdown fenced examples, not executable shell or Ruby backticks. The weak cryptography and network reconnaissance findings are also false positives based on ordinary raffle wording. Low residual risk remains because the skill claims cryptographic fairness but ships only instructions, so users must verify the runtime selection method.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur in fenced markdown examples that show prompts for selecting raffle winners. No executable script, shell command, Ruby code, or command interpolation is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown code fences or prompt text examples. There is no executable file or command invocation in the scanned skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Finding
low_findings.description: The flagged description discusses random winner selection for raffles. It does not implement a weak cryptographic algorithm or reference an unsafe hash or cipher.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive frontmatter, not code. No weak algorithm name or implementation appears at that location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Finding
low_findings.description: The flagged line says the process should be transparent. It contains no network scanning, host discovery, port scanning, or reconnaissance instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a feature bullet about transparency. There is no network target, tool, protocol, or scanning behavior.

low_findings.title: Unverifiable Fairness Claims
low_findings.description: The skill claims cryptographically secure randomness and no manipulation, but it provides no implementation that can be inspected. This is a documentation risk rather than evidence of malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fairness claims are explicit in the documentation. The repository contains only SKILL.md, so the claimed random selection implementation cannot be verified.

content.value_statement: Random drawings can be hard to explain and document. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through transparent raffle winner selection from lists, files, and sheets.
actual_capabilities: Guides random winner selection from plain text lists, CSV files, Excel files, and Google Sheets.
Supports one winner, multiple winners, runner-ups, and exclusion of previous winners.
Describes weighted selection based on an entries or ticket count column.
Prompts the assistant to report the total entry count, selected row, timestamp, and selection method.
Provides workflows for social giveaways, event raffles, team assignment, and participant selection.
limitations: The skill contains instructions only and does not include executable raffle selection code.
Google Sheets and local file access depend on the connected assistant tool permissions.
Users must verify eligibility rules, duplicate entries, and consent before announcing winners.
Cryptographic fairness depends on the assistant runtime or tools used during the drawing.

use_cases.title: Run a Subscriber Giveaway
use_cases.description: Select one or more winners from a subscriber list and produce a clear result summary.

use_cases.title: Draw Event Raffle Winners
use_cases.description: Pick winners and runner-ups from an attendee spreadsheet while excluding ineligible entries.

use_cases.title: Assign Participants Randomly
use_cases.description: Split participants into teams or select a sample for surveys, tests, or focus groups.

prompt_templates.title: Pick One Winner
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner raffle from a pasted list
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick one random winner from this list. Show the total entry count, selected entry, timestamp, and selection method.

prompt_templates.title: Pick Multiple Winners
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick 3 random winners from entries.csv. Do not select the same person twice. Include runner-ups if possible.

prompt_templates.title: Exclude Previous Winners
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick 2 winners from this spreadsheet. Exclude anyone listed as a previous winner and summarize the excluded entries.

prompt_templates.title: Run Weighted Drawing
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick one winner using the entries column as ticket weight. Explain the weighted method and provide an audit-friendly result.

output_examples.input: Pick one winner from a pasted list of newsletter subscribers.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports the number of valid entries, identifies the selected subscriber, records the timestamp, and states the random selection method.

output_examples.input: Pick five winners and three runner-ups from an event attendee spreadsheet.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns separate winner and runner-up sections, confirms duplicate prevention, and lists any entries excluded by the rules.

output_examples.input: Pick a weighted winner where each row has an entries count.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that higher entry counts increase probability, then reports the selected row and the total weighted ticket count.

best_practices: Clean the entry list before drawing and remove duplicates, test rows, and ineligible participants.
Save the timestamp, total entries, exclusions, and final result for audit records.
Pick runner-ups during the same session in case a selected winner cannot accept.
anti_patterns: Do not announce a winner before checking eligibility and duplicate entries.
Do not paste private contact data unless the assistant environment is approved for that data.
Do not claim legal or regulatory compliance from this skill alone.

faq.question: Does this skill include raffle drawing software?
faq.answer: No. It provides assistant instructions and workflows for selecting winners with available tools.

faq.question: Can it select winners from Google Sheets?
faq.answer: Yes, if the assistant has permission to access the sheet or the data is provided in the session.

faq.question: Can it prevent duplicate winners?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to exclude duplicate winners, but users should verify the cleaned entry list.

faq.question: Does it support weighted entries?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompts describe weighted selection based on an entries or ticket count column.

faq.question: Is the selection cryptographically secure?
faq.answer: The documentation requests secure randomness, but the actual security depends on the assistant tools used.

faq.question: Can I use it for regulated lotteries?
faq.answer: Use caution. This skill is not legal advice and does not verify lottery or contest compliance.
